items.title: Why comedian Nazeem Hussain laughs about the things that make him want to cry
items.summary: For comedian Nazeem Hussain, the best way to respond to war, discrimination and racism is to laugh about it. And he's built his whole career on this special brand of risky, outrageous comedy.

items.title: International Olympic Committee bans transgender athletes from women's events
items.summary: Under changes announced by the International Olympic Committee, the gender of athletes will be determined by a one-time gene-screening test.

items.title: Severe storm causes thousands of power outages and hundreds calls for help in Sydney
items.summary: Fire and Rescue NSW says there has been more than 400 calls for help, including one incident where a tree fell on a house trapping the occupant.

items.title: Israel plans to occupy southern Lebanon
items.summary: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

items.title: Qantas ramps up services to Europe as travellers avoid Middle East stopovers
items.summary: Qantas will increase capacity on flights between Australia and Europe after Easter, which experts say may see the "kangaroo route" evolve.

items.title: Rising fuel prices drive surge in demand for emergency support in Canberra
items.summary: Rising petrol and diesel prices are driving Canberra families to seek emergency assistance and forcing small businesses to consider whether to pass on higher costs to customers.

items.title: At 104, George Franklin is months away from becoming the world's oldest conductor
items.summary: WA musician George Franklin has already achieved a milestone by turning 104. He could soon garner another: the record for being the world's oldest band conductor.

items.title: Cherry-Evans has last laugh as Sea Eagles slump to third-straight defeat
items.summary: Daly Cherry-Evans is roundly booed on his return to Brookvale after his off-season switch from the Sea Eagles to the Roosters, as the tricolours dominate territory to claim 33-16 victory.

items.title: Thursday finance with Daniel Ziffer
items.summary: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

items.title: Sixteen-year-old girl the fifth person charged over death of Aidan Becker at Mernda railway station
items.summary: A 16-year-old girl is the fifth person to be charged following the death of a 22-year-old man at a train station in Melbourne's outer north earlier this month.

items.title: Last-touch non-call labelled 'farce' as Geelong edges Adelaide in thriller
items.summary: Geelong continues its streak over Adelaide at Kardinia Park, as one of the AFL's off-season rule changes is put squarely in the spotlight for the wrong reasons.

items.title: Sidon is facing a new wave of displaced residents from Lebanon's south as Israel's offensive continues
items.summary: Southern Lebanon has a "very long history" of conflict with Israel, and many of its residents remember past wars clearly. But they say the latest incursion feels different.

items.title: Why One Nation is not winning many lower house seats in the SA election despite its high vote
items.summary: One Nation has received more than 20 per cent of the primary vote in South Australia, and so far, its lower house seat count remains slight. Why is that, and why are onlookers so interested in the result?

items.title: Fuel dominates politics, Hanson confused over Rinehart flight questions
items.summary: The ongoing fuel crisis dominated politics this week but a probe into the National Anti-Corruption Commission was also announced and Pauline Hanson was grilled about accepting free flights from Gina Rinehart.

items.title: Funding for Macquarie Point precinct inches forward after key plans approved by federal government
items.summary: Federal funding for Hobart's waterfront redevelopment and stadium has reached a key milestone, with the Australian government accepting the site's master and housing plans.

items.title: Evacuations in Dubbo's CBD after crane collapses in powerful storm
items.summary: The crane was buffeted by a powerful storm before the boom came loose and fell onto a nearby apartment building.

items.title: Mount Alexander shire urges government to force fossil fuel companies to fund climate disaster recovery
items.summary: A Victorian council still cleaning up after a bushfire that wiped out more than 50 homes and businesses wants fossil fuel companies to pay for climate disaster recovery.

items.title: Risk of vegetable price rise in response to spiralling fuel and fertilizer costs
items.summary: A snap survey by AUSVEG reveals 27 per cent of Australian vegetable growers have already reduced planting, while some will not harvest current crops because rising costs make it unviable.

items.title: Germany's defence minister says Donald Trump has 'no exit strategy' in Iran
items.summary: Boris Pistorius says Germany will only help to secure the Strait of Hormuz once the US and Iran strike a ceasefire deal.

items.title: Socceroos approaching last chance saloon, with World Cup dreams in the balance
items.summary: The Socceroos' upcoming games against Cameroon and Curaçao could make or shatter World Cup dreams, and Socceroos legend Mark Schwarzer has backed the players, and coach Tony Popovic, to deliver come tournament time.

items.title: Centre for Disease Control issues health warning after outbreak of respiratory diphtheria in NT
items.summary: The Northern Territory's health department has declared an outbreak of respiratory diphtheria in the Top End.

items.title: Sydney private school teacher accused by police of grooming 14-year-old girl
items.summary: A casual teacher at Newington College has been charged with allegedly encouraging a 14-year-old girl to send sexually explicit images for money.

items.title: Bill to change emergency fuel laws and enable price cap passes Tasmanian Parliament
items.summary: A bill to give the Tasmanian government power to cap fuel prices has passed the state's parliament after a late-night sitting.

items.title: States to seek standardised reporting of fuel supply levels at national cabinet meeting
items.summary: State and territory leaders are also expected to push for a national approach to any further measures, including fuel rationing, if supply were to run short.

items.title: Amateur lobster fisher escapes attack by 3.5m great white shark off Cape Jaffa
items.summary: A fisher known as "Lucky Luke" to his mates lives up to his nickname after surviving a great white shark dragging him along the ocean floor while lobster fishing off the South Australian coast.

items.title: Federal government's ban on Iranian tourists to block 7,000 visa holders from Australia
items.summary: Thousands of Iranian travellers have now been barred from entering Australia under a government ban, prompting criticism by refugee advocates and some crossbenchers.

items.title: AI crocodile detection trials begin in north Queensland
items.summary: Researchers are trialling artificial intelligence to detect crocodiles in north Queensland waterways, but its success depends on human training to ensure accuracy.

items.title: AI detecting crocodiles in Queensland waterways
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: SA premier says regional areas will be prioritised for fuel deliveries amid global oil crisis
items.summary: South Australia's premier has assured farmers battling with soaring diesel prices that regional areas will be first to receive fuel deliveries.

items.title: AFL reveals new illicit drug policy that Andrew Dillon says is 'most stringent in Australian sport'
items.summary: The AFL says its new illicit drugs policy, which includes biannual hair-testing of all players, is the most stringent in Australian sport.

items.title: After a lopsided and uncertain start to the season, the NRL risks losing itself in a maze of set restarts
items.summary: After three rounds of the NRL season the increase in set restarts has left coaches, players and fans alike more confused than ever about the specifics behind rugby league's most controversial rule change.

items.title: AFL concussion class action expands to more than 100 former players and 11 clubs
items.summary: Eight more footballers have joined a multimillion-dollar concussion class action against the AFL, tipping the total to over 100 former players who all claim they have been left brain-damaged by the game.

items.title: Federal government urges rise in minimum wage above rate of inflation
items.summary: In its submission to the Fair Work Commission's annual wage review, the government again calls for the lowest-paid workers to not go backwards.

items.title: Government entities in Queensland unaware of cybersecurity vulnerabilities, audit office report finds
items.summary: The report found increasing frequency and sophistication of cyber attacks could expose government entities that had weak cybersecurity.

items.title: Leigh Ryswyk says reaction to being first openly gay AFL player 'overwhelming'
items.summary: Former Brisbane Lions player Leigh Ryswyk says the support he received after becoming the first AFL player to come out as gay has "blown me away".

items.title: ASX closes slightly down while NAB plans hundreds of job cuts — as it happened
items.summary: The ASX 200 has closed slightly down as Iran's foreign minister says the country will not negotiate with the US on a ceasefire plan. Brent crude remains above $US102 a barrel.

items.title: Over 300 petrol stations out of diesel, PM says 'nowhere is immune' to global crisis — as it happened
items.summary: During Question Time, Anthony Albanese faced a grilling on the impacts of war in the Middle East.

items.title: TGA proposes reforms to sunscreen regulation including new testing technologies
items.summary: The proposals include a number of changes to SPF testing requirements, oversight of the labs that do SPF testing, and the way SPF is labelled — reforms that would overhaul the sunscreen industry.

items.title: Hannah Gadsby, Rove McManus and Luke Heggie recommend comedians to see at the comedy festival
items.summary: Comedy season is in full swing, with festivals across the country hosting the best acts from Australia and the world. We asked our favourites to share their recommendations.

items.title: Sheffield Shield final: Alex Carey unbeaten as South Australia's top order rocked by Victoria before rain
items.summary: Victoria sent South Australia in to bat on day one of the Sheffield Shield final and claimed three wickets before rain stopped play.

items.title: PM calls second emergency national cabinet meeting over fuel crisis
items.summary: States and territory leaders will convene next week to further coordinate the national response to the fuel crisis as the fallout from the Iran war continues to escalate.

items.title: Law reform review calls for overhaul of WA's 'strictest' guardianship secrecy laws
items.summary: People living under a guardianship arrangement in WA can't publicly speak up, but a review says this has to change.

items.title: Sea the 'greatest risk and biggest asset' for beachside residents on Victoria's Bellarine and Barwon coasts
items.summary: For residents of Victoria's Bellarine Peninsula like Vicki, who is facing the impacts of climate change-induced erosion and inundation, the water near their doors is both their biggest asset and greatest risk.

items.title: Adelaide Crows coach Matthew Nicks remains positive as understrength side faces Cats in Geelong
items.summary: Matthew Nicks is imploring injury-hit Adelaide to "have a real crack" at ending the Crows' long-standing hoodoo in Geelong.

items.title: WA coast 'pummelled' as Cyclone Narelle could reach category five on approach
items.summary: Authorities in Western Australia say they are preparing for weather not seen since 2021, when a deadly cyclone flattened buildings in a town unaccustomed to such extreme conditions.

items.title: Stephen Colbert to co‑write new Lord of the Rings film after late-night show ends
items.summary: Talk show host Stephen Colbert, famous devotee to JRR Tolkien's Middle Earth, is co-writing a Lord of the Rings movie with his son.

items.title: Low-pressure system forecast to rapidly form in Tasman Sea
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: South Australia seek successive Sheffield Shield titles in final against Victoria
items.summary: Victory in this year's Sheffield Shield final over Victoria will be especially sweet for South Australia, with coach Ryan Harris saying their path to the title decider this time around has been challenging.

items.title: US says Iran seeking 'exit ramp' despite mixed ceasefire signals
items.summary: The White House says talks with Iran aimed at ending the conflict have not stalled, despite conflicting reports on Iran's response to a 15-point ceasefire proposal.

items.title: Our interest in electric vehicles has grown due to oil price spikes. And it's likely to remain
items.summary: The clear signal this trend data sends is that Australians are a pragmatic lot. If using an EV might save them money, then they are interested.

items.title: Former Brisbane Lion Leigh Ryswyk becomes first openly gay AFL player
items.summary: Former Brisbane Lions player Leigh Ryswyk has become the first openly gay AFL player, past or present.

items.title: Seasons clash as a polar blast joins summer cyclone on Australia's weather map
items.summary: A clash of seasons is developing on Australia's weather map this week as a tropical cyclone tracks towards the WA west coast and a polar blast with thunderstorms forms over the south-east.

items.title: The Gold Coast has transformed over 50 years. Its growth debate hasn't
items.summary: Fifty years after fears it would be a "concrete jungle" first surfaced, the Gold Coast is again grappling with the same question: how to grow without losing its identity.

items.title: Family launches palliative care foundation after 33-year-old son's final hospital experience
items.summary: Ryan Bowman fought through congenital heart defects and cancer, but his family says his final hospital visit led to some frightening final hours.

items.title: What we know and don't know about the Iran war negotiations
items.summary: US President Donald Trump's surprising claim that talks with Iran were yielding great progress only raised more confusion over a war whose goals were already unclear.

items.title: Queensland rising in battle against WA and SA for next AFL heartland
items.summary: The question of who loves footy the most has sparked countless hours of debate over the past century, and Queensland is staking a major claim.

items.title: This Mediterranean-style diet could keep your brain sharp as you age
items.summary: The food choices we make over decades — not just in later life, but across adulthood — may quietly shape the health of our brains in ways that only become visible much later.

items.title: From the Gulf War of the 1990s to the 2020 pandemic, global crises generally follow a predictable path
items.summary: If the mass uncertainty sparked by the military escalation in Iran is making you feel anxious, you are not alone — but there is some calming news for us all.

items.title: ABC Shakespeare quiz, parteth the seceund: Test thine knowledge of his plays once more
items.summary: Is this a quiz which you see before you? We're back with another 10 questions to test your knowledge of William Shakespeare's plays.

items.title: NSW forestry industry calls for secure future amid interstate shutdowns
items.summary: The NSW forestry industry is calling for secure access to timber as the proposed Great Koala National Park locks up resources and recent closures of Victorian and Western Australian sectors raise fears for the future of native logging.

items.title: Will Kyle Sandilands really sue radio network ARN?
items.summary: There's an $88 million question doing the rounds of legal and media circles, after the catastrophic falling-out between radio shock jocks Kyle and Jackie O and their former radio network ARN.

items.title: Iran reviews ceasefire plan as Gulf states say Middle East war poses 'existential threat'
items.summary: Strikes have not let up across the Middle East with targets in Iran, Israel, Lebanon, Bahrain, Kuwait, Jordan and Saudi Arabia all coming under fire.

items.title: Australian shares soar on Middle East ceasefire hopes and softer inflation figures
items.summary: The local share market has its best day in almost a year after reports the US is seeking a ceasefire with Iran buoyed investor sentiment, and monthly inflation was softer than expected.

items.title: NASA pivots to moon base, nuclear propulsion in expanded multi-billion-dollar program
items.summary: NASA has cancelled plans to deploy a space station in lunar orbit and will instead build a $28.7 billion base on the moon's surface.

items.title: Foreign Minister Penny Wong warns Israel against south Lebanon occupation
items.summary: Australia warns Israel against occupying parts of Lebanon, urging it to respect international law after the displacement of more than 1 million people.

items.title: Shepparton man spared jail after leaving child in cardboard box during car theft
items.summary: A 32-year-old man says he is "ashamed" of his actions after he left a child in a cardboard box in a regional Victorian car park while stealing the vehicle the toddler was in.

items.title: Questions over deal between AI company Firmus and Tasmania's power generator
items.summary: Questions remain about the deal struck to supply power to an AI company in Tasmania's north, not long after a request to supply another manufacturer was rejected.
